"Follow the Dall, Not the Muft - 'Allmah Muqbil bin Hdi, Allah have mercy on him

al-Allmah Muqbil bin Had al-Wdi, Allah have mercy on him, was asked regarding the befitting stance of a person on a fatwa from a single scholar; specifically in matters of disagreement. Should it suffice him or should he ask as many scholars as possible, and then follow a fatwa from their fatwa? Is this acceptable and is it negligent to ask only a single scholar? 

His answer:

What I advise the brother with, if he is a student of knowledge, is that he should research in the books, and should diligently attempt to find the truth himself from the books of the people of knowledge, and the affair is easy, Allah willing. And if this is not easy, then what matters is the ease of the heart. So if the heart is assured by asking the first scholar [whose answers come] with proof [then this is acceptable]. 

You must ask for proof, because Allah says, Follow what has been revealed to you from your Lord and do not follow other than Him any awliy. Little do you remember.  [Surat al-Arf: 3]. If he brings proof from the book of Allah and from the sunnah of the Messenger of Allah, sallallhu alaihi wa sallam, and you have understood the proof, then suffice yourself with this, or then you can ask another. And be sure that the proof is not abrogated, or that it is not a proof for what it is being used for, as it may be that the muft takes to a madh-hab, and is giving a fatwa which agrees with his madh-hab.

Ijbatus-Sil, p.638
Translation: Abu Hafsah Kashiff Khan"

"[An Example from the Salaf in Humility, Confidence, and Academic Rigour]

It is mentioned in 'The Echelons of the Grammarians' of Az-Zubaidi, that it was said to Abu Ubaid [Al-Qsim Ibn Sallm d. 222 AH]: 

"Indeed so-and-so says: 'Ab Ubaid has erred in two hundred words brought in his book {Al-Gharb Al-Musannaf}.'" 

So Ab Ubaid was calm and forbearant, and he did not disparage the man in any way. Instead he said: "In the Musannaf are so many thousands of entries, so if I haven't erred except in this small quantity, then it is no large amount. But if this companion of ours would present himself so we could debate him regarding these 200 errors  according to his claim  perhaps we could have found valid justifications for them."

Az-Zubaidi added: "Then I counted the number of entries in the book, and my tally arrived at 17,770 words."

[Kitb Tabaqt Al-Mufassirn lid-Dwudi, 2/42]

And Ab Mas'd Ar-Rz said: "Twelve or thirteen individuals impugned [Al-Hfidh] Rawh ibn Ubdah, yet none of their speech was upheld." 

[Tahdhb At-Tahdhb, 4/391]"

"A Warning To Those That Defend Themselves But Are Silent When The Truth & It's People Are Violated 

Ibn Qayyim    said: 

"And what religion, and what good, is there in a person who sees that which Allah has prohibited being violated, His boundaries being ignored and transgressed upon, His religion being abandoned, and the Sunnah of His Messenger being rejected? Yet his heart is unmoved, and his tongue remains silent. Indeed, he is a mute devil, just like one who speaks with falsehood is an articulate devil. 

Has the religion (of Allah) ever been tested with anything other than the likes of these people? Those who, so long as their food and (positions of) leadership are secured and protected, have no concern for what happens to the religion of Allah. The best among them may feel slight sadness, but should he be challenged regarding something he holds dearwhether concerning his status or his wealthhe will strive and toil to the utmost of his ability, using the three levels of rejection and rebuke to defend himself, (i.e. they use their hands, their tongues, and their heart to; change, repel, reject, and rebuke that evil). 

These individuals, though they have fallen in the sight of Allah and are under His anger, have been tested in this worldly life with the greatest of trials, yet they do not feel it. Certainly, for sure, it is the death of the hearts. For indeed, the more alive the heart is, the stronger its anger is for the sake of Allah and His Messenger, and the more complete its defence of the religion shall be."

['Ilm al-Muwaqq'n 'an Rabb al-'lamn Ibn al-Qayyim 2/177] translated by Abu Ukkashah Abdulhakeem"

"Who was ash-Shaykh, al-Allmah, Ubayd al-Jbir?

He is the Shaykh and Allmah, Ubayd bin Abd Allh bin Sulaymn al-Jbir al-Hamadn, and Ban Jbir is among the tribes of arb of the ijz.
He was born in the town of al-Faqr in al-Far valley in the region of Madnah in 1357 AH (1938). At the age of eight, he moved with his father to Mahd al-Dhahab, where he acquired the first stages of his education. His father moved there in order to work. He remained in education at this location for eight years. After that, he returned to al-Far valley. Then in 1376 AH (1956) he settled in Madnah and, due to family circumstances, was unable to study for a period of time.

Then in the year 1381 AH (1961), he resumed his studies, starting with Dr al-adth al-Madaniyyah. Then he joined al-Mahad al-Ilm and stayed there for five years. He got married before joining the Faculty of Sharah in the Islamic University of Madnah in 1388 AH (1968). He graduated four years later in 1392 AH (1972), excelling in his grade.
From his teachers through the course of his studies include: Shaykh Sayf al-Ramn bin Amad, Shaykh Ammr bin Abd Allh, Shaykh Abd al-Ramn bin Abd Allh al-Ajln, Shaykh umayd al-zim, Shaykh ammd al-Anr and Shaykh Abd al-Musin al-Abbad.

After that, he was an imm in Masjid al-Sabt in Madnah between 1387-1392 AH. Then he was a teacher at Umar bin Abd al-Aziz Intermediate School in Jeddah from 1392 AH to 1396 AH. After that, the Shaykh worked as a caller for Markaz al-Dawah wal-Irshd, assisting the director of the centre, Shaykh Abd al-Azz bin Bz, in his presence and acting on his behalf in his absence from the end of 1396 AH to 1404 AH (1976-1983).
Then he was a teacher at the Islmic University between the end of 1407 AH to 1414 AH. During his presence there, he also attained a Masters in the subject of tafsr. After a few years serving at the Community Service Centre affiliated with the Islmic University, the Shaykh then retired in 1417 AH (1996).

After this the Shaykh continued his lectures and lessons, with students from the Islmic University frequenting his home and benefiting from his knowledge, advice and wisdom. During this period, the Shaykh also aided the Salafi dawah tremendously, particularly in the West by way of fatherly advice, clarifications, admonitions and refutations of the people of falsehood and their false innovated principles. The Shaykh had a very strong relationship with the students of knowledge from Maktabah Salafiyyah, Birmingham. The Shaykh has many beneficial works in the field of aqdah, manhaj, and fiqh. Shaykh Rab bin Hd (may Allh have mercy on him) said of the Shaykh: Shaykh Ubayd is from the esteemed scholars of the Salafs, and he is known for piety, abstinence and speaking with truth.

Shaykh Ubayd was also praised and commended by other Major Scholars such as Shaykh Amad al-Najm and Shaykh Zayd al-Madkhal (may Allh have mercy on them).
The Shaykh (may Allh have mercy on him) passed away at the age of 84 on 28 Rab al-Thn 1444 AH, corresponding to 22 November 2022. May Allh shower him with mercy and reward him greatly for his service to Islm, the Muslims and the Salaf dawah.

"The Danger of Free-Mixing and a Warning to Those Who Treat the Matter Lightly

Ab Hurayrah narrated that the Messenger of Allh (   ) said:

The best rows for men are the first rows, and the worst are the last rows, and the best rows for women are the last rows, and the worst are the first rows. Muslim 440.

Al-Nawaw comments:

The reason the last rows are preferred for women who are present with men is because they are farther from mixing with the men, seeing them, becoming attached in the heart due to seeing their movements or hearing their speech, and similar matters. For this reason, the first rows are considered the opposite (less preferable) for women, and Allh knows best.

If this concern applies even in the most sacred of places, where hearts are softened, desires are restrained by the fear of Allh, and one is surrounded by righteous believers, yet unnecessary mixing between men and women is still cautioned against, then what about places of lesser sanctity, where temptation is stronger, heedlessness is more common, and restrain is weaker?

"Dismissing The Scholars for Lacking Expertise in Nawzil  A Surr Principle Repacked

Shaykh @Arafatbinhassan Al-Muammad, may Allh preserve him was asked: 

Question: May Allh reward you, noble Shaykh. A questioner asks: What is meant by a Nzilah?

Answer: A Nzilah is an event that affects the Muslims at large, requiring the senior scholars to examine it. They study its reality, how it has arisen, what aspects relate to the Sharah, and what ruling (fatw) applies. An example is COVID. It was a widespread pandemic that affected Muslims everywhere, so scholars had to determine the appropriate rulings. A Nzilah is thus a new occurrence for people, often involving hardship, and scholars must clarify the rulings that apply to the Muslims in such a situation.

They first understand it accurately, identify the point of dispute, research it, compare it with similar cases, then consult one another and issue a ruling.

Issues like mosques, distancing, masks, mixing or avoiding mixing, all of these are religious matters. So whom do we ask? We ask the specialists, and they are the scholars.

When the plague struck the Levant (Ash-Shm), what did Umar do? He gathered the scholars. He met with the Immigrants (Muhjirn), then the Supporters (Anr), then the elders of Quraysh who had accepted Islam at the Conquest (of Makkah). He listened to all of them and reached a conclusion. He was the Leader of The Believers (Amrul-Muminn), and when he gives a command, it must be obeyed.

When he gave his ruling, one Companion disagreedAb Ubaydah ibn Al-Jarr, who was the governor over the Levant in the place where the plague was, and he died because of this plague, may Allh be pleased with him. He said: O Leader of The Believers, are you fleeing from the decree of Allh? Umar replied: If only someone other than you had said it, O Ab Ubaydah. Meaning: with your knowledge and virtue, you say such a statement? If someone else had said it, we might have accepted it from him, but from you we do not accept that you say this. Yeswe are fleeing from the decree of Allh to the decree of Allh.

As Umar explained, Ab Ubaydah accepted it. Then Abdur-Ramn ibn Awf arrived and said: I have knowledge of this, and he mentioned the adth: if you hear of a plague in a land, do not enter it, and if you are in it, do not leave. Thus Umars decision aligned with the Sunnah.

In such matters, we return to the scholars.

Now some of them have appeared who say: do not return to the scholars because they do not understand medicine. What benefit is there in referring to them? This is dangerous speech! This effectively dismisses the scholars rulings in Nawzil. Do you even realize what you are saying? Because when you say, Do not return to the scholars in medical matters since they do not understand medicine, then finedoes any of us claim that Shaykh Al-Fawzn is a medical, agricultural or technical expert?

Based on the fatw of the one who made this statement, then we should not return to Al-Fawzn in any Nzilah, because he does not understand medicine, nor agriculture, nor computers, nor satellite technology, and so on.

What is the conclusion of this? That we do not return to the scholars in Nawzil. The conclusion of such thinking is to abandon the scholars and turn instead to doctors and engineers. This is dangerous speech!

Moreover, he did not stop at that. He also says: Likewise, they do not go beyond the statement of the rulerif the ruler says something, they will say we hear and obey. Is this the way of our scholars? That whatever the ruler says, they hear and obey? This is false and incorrect. If the ruler makes a mistake, they do not obey himthere is no obedience to creation in disobedience to the Creator. If it is an error, they advise the ruler and speak to him (privately), because this is the methodology of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jamah. 

continued"

"So we rejected this statement and said: this is a dangerous statement. Its implication is that you are nullifying the Fatw of the scholars in Nawzil. If you see that there is a mistake, and you are a Salaf who respects the scholars and also respects the rulers, then write to them. Present your viewpoint only. But to establish this as a general principlethis is false and wrong! 

This resembles the approach of the Surrs and Qubs during the Gulf crisis, who said: Our scholars do not understand current affairswe understand current affairs. America entered, Europe did such-and-such, and you, O scholarslike ibn Bzyou are only with the rulers, obeying them. The same wordsonly the people and circumstances have changed. There it was a Gulf crisis related to the entry of disbelievers (to the lands of the Muslims); here it is a COVID crisis related to medicine.

Furthermore, this man (making these claims) works in medicine. If we look at his specialization, do you work in every field of medicine and every specialty? No. Then speak only within your level. That is one point.

Secondly, do you want the entire Ummah to abandon the doctors of the world and the consultants, and instead follow your opinion? This is dangerous speech! It is unclear what this person intends with such statements. 

Despite that, he has been advised, and we have advised him before. We even sent to him years ago when he issued a Fatw. Look at this Fatwmany people may not even be aware of it. He issued a ruling that social distancing was Minor Shirk. That means Al-Fawzn has fallen into Shirk, the Muft has fallen into Shirk, and our scholars such as Ubayd and others have fallen into Shirk. And we, the students of knowledge, have all fallen into Shirk. And the Fatw of the scholars who permitted distancing are, according to him, Fatw of Shirk.

So who is behind you that you speak with such words? What do you want, that you strike at the scholars by claiming they have fallen into (minor) Shirk, that they should not be referred to, and that they neither understand nor comprehend? This is what he says in the audio recordings. 

Some have not even listened to these recordings yet and do not want to listenand this is a calamity. You find a person plunging headlong into Fitnah while knowing nothing. And if you say to him, Come, let us sit and clarify things for you, he refuses to listen. This is blind partisanship. 

As for us, we clarify the error. Whoever is guided, then all praise is due to Allh, and whoever goes astray, then it is upon himself.

Shaykh Abdul-Azz ibn Bz was asked on his official site: Should we refer only to doctors, or to the scholars? He replied: No. One must return to the scholar. If the matter were purely medical, then yeswe would return to the doctors; because they are the people of specialization. But this concerns a matter connected to medicine and also to the Sharah.

Does Al-Fawzn immediately issue a ruling when a purely medical issue comes to him? You think highly of yourself and consider yourself an expert. But when this scholarAl-Fawzn, the Muft, Al-Abbd, Rab, and othersare asked about a worldly technical matter such as medicine or agriculture, do they refer to specialists or not? They do refer to the specialists. This shows that you do not understand how the scholars issue Fatw.

"Poignant & Fatherly Advice Forwarded from Shaikh Abu Hafsah Kashiff Khan (may Allh preserve him upon good):

 In the name of Allah.

The most noble with Allah is the one who has the most Taqwa (Surat al-Hujurat: 13), and Taqwa is a direct result of knowledge (Surat al-Fatir: 28), and for this reason Allah raises the scholars levels above the rest of the believers (Surat al-Mujdilah: 11). It is taqwa that raises a persons rank with Allah, not a title that they are given by the creation, such as Imm, Allmah, Shaykh, or Ustdh. Rather, these descriptions are given to help understand a persons level of knowledge by other scholars, and they are not something that inherently elevate a persons status in the sight of Allah. At times, scholars may differ regarding a persons level of knowledge, but this differing does not change their true status with Allah, which is based upon Taqwa.

I have always, and Allah is my witness, advised with respect for all those that are recognized for their knowledge and benefitting the ummah, especially the scholars. I have done this in private and public settings. I have done this in the United States and the United Kingdom and in Pakistan. Even if I disagree with a scholars opinion or believe they have erred in their ijtihd, I have always advised with respecting their honor, and disagreeing while maintaining manners with them.

In this present affair, may Allah remove it from us, we have heard unbefitting speech regarding Shaykh al-Fawzn, Shaykh Abdul Muhsin al-Abbd, Shaykh Hassan al-Banna, Shaykh Ali al-Hudhayf, Shaykh Abdullah al-Bukhari, and other scholars and those less than them. I have advised against all of it, privately and publicly. I wrote some days ago, Dangerous is the precedence of accepting tabd based on personaliities instead of proofs. My words were general so that they remain applicable to whosoever falls into this affair, regardless of who is declared an innovator or the one declaring it.

But

I, clearly and with no ambiguity, do not support selective anger, and demands to write support and defend a single scholar, basing our wal-wal-bar on whether you support that one group of scholars. I have heard over the last year well known scholars and teachers being referred to or implied to be jhil, fake, lacking competence, undedicated to dawah, mubtadi, and much more. But I did not see a single writing defending Shaykh Hassan al-Banna or Shaykh Abdul Muhsin, or other than them. I did not demand from anyone to write or inform me of their stance on Shaykh Ali al-Hudhayf, and then formulated my stance against them or for them based on their response.

As for my private advices, then there is no legislated obligation upon me to make them public just as their was no legislated obligation on Usmah, radiyallhu anhu, to make public his advice to Uthmn radiyallhu anhu. I have no obligation to sign a Bayn/Mawqif/Nashah when I can advise in a more intimate and private manner if and when I see fit to do so. How many lectures have we given mentioning the patience of Shaykh Rab with the people of innovation? A better example is Nuh alayhis-salm who was patient with his people for 950 years. Your ijtihd on how to advise is not binding on me, nor an indication of guilt in anything.

Let it be known that even those that I have deeply disagreed with, Allah is my witness, that I have not blocked a single of them. I still speak with them when the opportunity arises.

As for me visiting Maktabah Salafiyyah or TROID, then I have known them for close to 30 years. I have lectured there alongside others on many occasions. Then they are, all of them, my brothers. We agree and we disagree. We advise and we argue and we remain brothers. There is a not a single one of them except that we have at some point disagreed, some more than others as of late. This has been my relationship with all of my brothers, and will continue to be so for those that wish to continue their relationships with me.

In closing, I advise my brothers everywhere to ask for Allahs protection, fear your sins, and busy yourselves with that.

As for the fitnah, then allow the scholars to deal with it. Do not busy yourselves, your families, and your communities with this, and Allahs protection is sought.

Your brother,
Abu Hafsah Kashiff Khan
Maryland, USA"

"My Advice to Christian Social Media Influencers Who Are Beginning to Recognise the Truth About Geo-Politicsand the Fact that True Muslims Want Good For People, Not Harm.

The Bible that many Christians read is very clear regarding Gods First Commandment: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the land of slavery. You shall have no other gods besides Me. (Exodus 20:2-3) This is the God of Moses (peace be upon him). It was He who sent Jesus as His servant and Messenger (peace be upon him and his mother), and not as a god to be worshipped.

The Bible explicitly states, You shall not make for yourselves idols, nor shall you set up for yourselves an image or a sacred pillar, nor shall you place a figured stone in your land to bow down to it, for I am the Lord your God. (Leviticus 26:1) Yet, in how many places across the world do we see statues and images portraying Mary with baby Jesus in her arms, or statues of Christ himself in churches and elsewhere, some stretching metres high and others as small handmade figurines? 

We see thousands of people gathering to prostrate themselves before these images and statues, seeking blessings from them. This is the very idolatry forbidden in scripture and by sound reason, which nullifies claims to monotheism. 

Furthermore, the Bible commands, You shall have no other gods before me. You shall not make for yourselves any graven image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth: You shall not bow down yourselves to them, nor serve them.(Exodus 20:4) 

God commands similarly in the Quran: And from His signs are the night and the day, and the Sun and Moonso do not prostrate to the Sun or the Moon but prostrate to God alone, the one who created them if it is He whom you truly [claim to] worship. (Qurn 41:37) 

In the New Testament, the First Commandment is reiterated: Jesus declared, Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind. This is the first and greatest commandment. (Matthew 22:37-38)

Jesus was a sincere and humble servant of God, as we read in the Qurn:And behold! God will say on the Day of Judgment: O Jesus, son of Mary, did you say unto men, Worship me and my mother as gods instead of Allh? Jesus will respond: Glory to You! Never could I say what I had no right to say. Had I said such a thing, You would indeed have known it, for You know what is in my innerself, though I know not what is in YoursYou know all that is hidden. Never did I say to them anything except what You commanded me to say: Worship Almighty God, my Lord and your Lord. (5:116-117) 

Even in the Bible, there are clear statements showing that Jesus (peace be upon him) would direct worship to Almighty God alone. Here is a verse from the Bible read by millions of Christian Arabs:And Jesus said to him: Why do you call me good? No one is good except Allh alone!(Mark 10:18). Here, Jesus distinguishes himself from God, i.e., that he is the worshipper and God is the One deserving of worship, alone without partners. Furthermore, God states in the Qurn: They have disbelieved who say: God is the Messiah son of Mary. (Quran 5:72) And: They have disbelieved who say: God is the third of three [in a Trinity]. (5:73) God is One, alone deserving of worship, and the Prophets are His faithful servants.  

In the Old Testament of the Arabic Bible, we find the following:In the beginning, Allh created the Heavens and the Earth. (Genesis, verse 1). And in the Qurn: Say: He is Allh (God), the One. God is the Self-Sufficient whom all creatures need, He begets not, nor was He begotten. (112:1-4) 

The final Messenger of God to mankind, Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him), said: Do not exaggerate in praising me as the Christians have exaggerated the praise of Jesus, son of Mary, for I am only a servant, so say, He is the servant of God and His Messenger. (Al-Bukhr, no. 3445)

Muslims believe that Jesus is a Prophet of God, born from a virgin birth, from a noble mother, Mary, who was untouched by any man. The Angels said to her:O Mary, verily God gives you the glad tidings of a word spoken by Him. His name will be the Messiah Jesus, son of Mary, held in honour in this world and in the Hereafter, and of those brought near to God.(Qurn 3:45) In defence of the accusations against his mother, God promised Mary that Jesus (peace be upon him) would speak whilst still a baby, He will speak to the people in the cradleand in manhood, and he will be one of the righteous. (Quran 3:46)

So, learn this Religion, study it sincerely, and you will find the Truth, God-willing.

"This weekend, I discussed the call of Shaykh Abd Allah al-Qarawi. Before his arrival in the southern region of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, he purchased 20,000 rials worth of books, including Kitab al-Tawhid, Al-Usul al-Thalathah, masahif, and others. One of the first things he did after settling was open a small shop to sell his wares. He also established lessons there. Students came to learn the fundamentals of the faith. That small shop became the fountainhead of his call in the region.

This shows that the most effective dawah can spread from the simplest places. While many of our centers and masjids may be humble, they are beacons in cities across the country. I am reminded of this each time I visit our brothers at @Masjid_IbnAbbas in College Park, Georgia. From that masjid in a strip mall, our brothers have established the call to Salafiyyah. May Allah bless them for their efforts. It always brings us joy to visit them. This weekend was no exception.

We gathered, travelers and residents, to read passages from the classical work Ighathah al-Lahfan by Imam Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah, a work that clarifies the devils deceptions. Al-Allamah Salih al-Fawzan likens it to Ibn al-Jawzis Talbis Iblis. Both works clarify the deviations of misguided groups, such as the Sufis and the people of rhetoric. The English version, Purification of the Heart, is a translation of the books introduction, in which the author discusses how to protect the heart from the diseases of doubts and desires, how to arm it against deviation and disbelief. 

It was a wonderful three-day event, full of warm encounters and benefit. May Allah reward the community for their invitation and hospitality. May He continue to bless them to spread the Salafi call in the Atlanta area. Indeed, Allah is merciful to His servants."

""What Is the Definition of a Nzilah and What Are Its Elements?

As for epidemics and new diseases, then they do not come under the nzilah as defined, as they have occurred throughout history, but in a more general sense, they are a crisis, so they are a nzilah in a more general sense. These types of events are addressed in an administrative and logistical manner, similar to calamities in general, such as a flood, or an earthquake, with the involvement of the relevant authorities.

Throughout history, there have been plagues and epidemics, and physicians and specialists have differed with each other in how to treat the disease and the epidemic in general, however, no one censored or silenced physicians and specialists with the claim that these affairs are from the nawzil which only rulers and scholars speak about. No one forced physicians or specialists to accept and operate upon a particular medical theory or school or condemned them for having their views. These are medical issues, and medicine is at minimum permissible, and recommended at best.

In this insightful segment, Shaykh Abul-Hasan Mlik discusses the importance of repetition and disciplined study in the tradition of seeking knowledge.

The discussion begins with the remarkable example of Shaykh Hfidh al-Hakami ( ), who was known for his extraordinary memorization. An astonishing account is mentioned in which he memorized an entire book in a single night through intense repetition and review, later demonstrating his mastery by answering detailed questions from it.

The clip then transitions into the methodology practiced by the scholars of the Salaf, emphasizing that true memorization and understanding come from constant repetition and revisiting lessons rather than passive reading.

Shaykh Abul-Hasan also explains how Shaykh Abdullah al-Qarw ( ) would carefully test his students to ensure they truly understood what they had learned and retained it long-term. Through mentorship, encouragement, and structured learning, he helped cultivate exceptional students of knowledge.

This powerful reminder highlights the discipline, repetition, and teacher-student relationship that helped preserve and transmit sacred knowledge.

"The Companions Were Upon Five Matters

Imaam al-Awzaa'ee (d. 157H) narrated from Hassan b. 'Atiyyah - may Allaah have mercy upon them both - that he said:

"The companions of Muhammad () and those who followed them upon goodness (at-Taabi'oon) were upon five matters;

 adhering strictly to the Jamaa'ah,
 following the Sunnah,
 the building of Masaajid,
 recital of the Qur'aan
 and Jihaad (striving) in Allaah's Path."

Reference: Ta'dheem Qadr as-Salaah of Imaam Muhammad b. Nasr al-Marwazee (d.294H) (no.745), Sharh Usool I'tiqaad Ahlus-Sunnah of Imaam al-Laalikaaee (no.48) & Miftaahul-Jannah of as-Suyootee (p.64-65).

"Major news for Makkah: Airport plans moving forward 

Saudi Arabia is pushing ahead with a new airport in Makkah as part of wider transport upgrades aimed at transforming the pilgrim experience.

A new airport in Makkah has been officially approved at a strategic level, marking a significant step forward in improving accessibility for millions of visitors each year.

The airport is planned to be built to international standards, designed to accommodate the growing number of Hajj and Umrah pilgrims travelling to the holy city.

Importantly, the project will involve private sector partnerships, ensuring long-term sustainability and efficiency in development and operations.

Authorities have also confirmed that the airport will be developed in a way that complements existing infrastructure, including King Abdulaziz International Airport in Jeddah - not competing with it.

This is part of a wider transformation of transport in Makkah, alongside metro expansions and major road upgrades aimed at easing congestion and improving movement across the city.

This development aligns with Saudi Arabias long-term vision to enhance the overall Hajj & Umrah experience for millions around the world.

Big changes are coming to Makkah.

"A Note Regarding my Trip to the USA 8th - 13th April, 2026

It has once again been a truly enjoyable visit to the USA. I extend my sincere thanks to our beloved communities in Roanoke, Virginia, and Columbus, Ohio, for organizing and facilitating the events over the past few days. I am deeply grateful for their outstanding hospitality.

By the blessing of Allh, I had the opportunity to meet communities being cultivated upon the way of the Scholarshonouring them, loving them, and recognizing their rightful status. May Allh continue to grant them success.

The dawah in the USA is clearly growing. I ask Allh to bless the communities of Roanoke and Columbus, and all Salaf communities, as well as all our respected and noble brothersthe honourable dut and teachers in the USA who strive tirelessly to benefit the Muslims across the country. They teach the Quran and Sunnah, connect the people to the Scholars, and nurture within them love and respect for them: Ab Muhammad al-Maghrab, Abul Hasan Mlik, Hasan Somali, Abul-Abbs Ms Richardson, Anwar Wright, Umar Quinn, Rasheed Barbee, Raha Batts, Jaml Finch, Hamza AbdurRazzq, Mustaf George, Ab Ansa Abdul Hakeem, and others among the teachers upon the same commendable path whom I may have missed.

For decades, our dawah in the West has been firmly built upon returning to the Scholarsmaintaining strong ties with them, seeking their guidance in our affairs, and recognizing our position beneath them. By the permission of Allh, if we remain steadfast upon this path, we will continue to grow and progress, our dawah will only become stronger, and we will find the sweetness of brotherhood, love and unity between us.

As for those who are no longer able to uphold this way, and have instead opened doors to corruption and misplaced boldness against the scholars that we long worked within the dawah to keep firmly shut for yearsand in fact rebuked opposers who often attempt to open themthen do not concern yourselves with such individuals or their supporters issuing lengthy and meandering justifications for their weak and erroneous positions lacking resolveat a time when nothing but firm resolve itself is the requirement.

Ab Muadh Taqweem

The state you live on is the state you die on

Consistency?

Perseverance?

Rare.

Allah obligated submission upon His slaves, not chasing desires.

You follow evidences, not whimswhether the issue is disputed or agreed upon.

Reflect.

Scary how Muslims are relying on AI apps like ChatGPT for Islamic knowledge instead of going to scholars.

These apps need to be fact-checkedthey even make up Qurn verses!

@MuslimCouncil shared a khutbah quoting a verse that doesnt exist.



Wake up!

Muftis & teachers must approach fiqh rulings with balanceharshness in issues where scholars differed only breeds confusion & poor practice.

When a matter doesnt oppose a clear text or consensus, present it with respect.

Wisdom lies in balanced discourse, not rigid harshness.

#Ramadan is slipping awayits final breaths are upon us.

If you excelled, this is your moment to seal it with glory.

If you wasted it, fall on your face in repentance before the door shuts in your face.

Its not how you startedits how you end.

People chase healing in every corner of the world, yet the cure sits untouched on their shelf: the #Quran.

They cry for relief while ignoring the very Book that heals hearts.

Lostnot for a lack of guidance, but for turning away from it.

Du is the weapon of both the strong and the weak, the refuge of prophets and the chosen ones.

Through it, they ward off every calamity and seek the mercy of their Lord.

So, never underestimate its powerwhat is beyond your reach is never beyond your du.

Do not waste this blessed month getting caught up in drama.

Your book of deeds is filling up.

Dont let it be with nonsense.

Focus on what actually matters.

#Ramadan2025
Dont let petty disputes cost you the forgiveness of Allah youre in desperate need of.

O Allah, have mercy on those who fasted with us in past Ramadhns but are now beneath the earth.

They once longed for its nights with us, and now they await our du in their graves.

O Allah, forgive them, ease their loneliness, and grant them Paradise.

One of the greatest dangers for a believer is forgetting his sins.

Sins have consequences, even if they come years later.

Yet, when calamity strikes, some are baffledWhat did I do?

They forgot, but the record never does.

This is why Allah commanded us with #Istighfr.

The one who abandons sin only in #Ramadhn hasnt truly repentedhes just pausing his disobedience for a season.

Yes, hell be rewarded for honouring the sacred month.

But lets be clear: this isnt Tawbah.

True repentance isnt a 30-day contractits a lifelong commitment.

The Ashari view of Gods actions is rooted in a strict form of divine command theory (DCT), where no logically possible act is deemed unbefitting of Him. This approach raises deeper questions about the nature of truth and divine guidance. 

For example, on the Ash'ari view, Does God have to tell the truth? This question gets to the core of a deep problem that underlies their strict DCT view. 

Ill break it down in simple terms here. But first, a disclaimer:

                .               .

 B:

- Asharis believe that no logically possible act is unbefitting of God. Any action you can possibly conceive of is an equally possible and valid expression of His will as any other. On this view, there is nothing about God that makes the creation of this world any more likely than a world with nothing but a pile of dog feces created for absolutely no reason (excuse the stark example, but it helps illustrate the point clearly). He chose to create this world instead of the feces world for literally no reason at all.

- For example, on this view, a world that contains nothing but an innocent baby suffering in Hell for an eternity for absolutely no reason is perfectly befitting of God and His perfection. It is axiologically identical to a world where the righteous are rewarded and only the sinful are punished. Both possible realities are equally befitting of God, and theres absolutely no reason or wisdom that can explain why He chose to do one instead of the other.

- Even in terms of our deeds: God doesnt prefer one deed over another, unless by prefer you mean He just arbitrarily willed for it to happen (for no reason). The most pious worshipper to have ever lived is identical to Iblis in terms of Gods preference or love. Unless we understand love as Gods will to reward the worshipper, which again, is completely arbitrary and could have gone in the exact opposite direction with the worshiper getting punished and Iblis getting rewarded. Both equally possible and equally befitting of God. The only reason we assume that one is better than the other is because thats what weve become accustomed to.

So, on the Ashari view, Gods actions are completely arbitrary. He doesnt act for any specific reason. He creates clouds through which He creates rain, but He didnt create the clouds for that reason. It just happens to be the case that he created clouds followed by rain.

  

So, where does the truthfulness question come in?

Well, if all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God, and theres nothing about Him that explains why He chooses to act one way instead of another except for brute arbitrary will, then how can you be sure He tells the truth?

They cant appeal to perfection, as Al-Juwayni and others explain, because then the entire foundation collapses and we could (as we do) similarly appeal to perfection when explaining why any of the above possibilities is unbefitting of God.

The typical Ashari move here is to argue that its logically impossible for God to lie. They say that Gods created speech (like the Arabic Qur'an) necessarily indicates His internal speech.

Therefore, lying is impossible because created speech cannot conflict with the internal meaning it indicates.

But even if thats true, it doesnt solve the real issue, namely: How do you know  that this SPECIFIC instance of "created" speechlike the Qur'ancorresponds to Gods internal speech?

  " "

In response, Asharis would typically appeal to miracles. 

The idea is that when you see a prophet perform miracles, God creates an unshakable certainty in your mind that the prophet is truthful.

The logic is that miracles are like a seal of approval from God proving that the prophet speaks the truth.

They use the common analogy of the king to explain this:

Imagine a king in a grand gathering. A man stands up and says, Im the kings messenger and will speak on behalf of him. To prove it, the man turns to the king and says, O King, if Im truthful, stand up and sit back down as a sign. When the king, who doesnt usually do such a thing, actually complies, everyone in the room instantly knows the man is truthful.

Asharis argue that this analogous to what happens when God displays miracles through a prophet. It creates undeniable certainty that the prophet is truthful.

And of course, this is true in general, but not on Ashari grounds!

Why? Because they also believe that God acts for no reasons whatsoever and that any logically conceivable act is in line with His perfection.

This analogy only works if the king acts with purpose; if he has "reasons" behind his actions. If the kings actions are just randomif he stands up and sits down for no reason whatsoeverthen the act doesnt prove anything.

He couldn't have done it with the intention of demonstrating the mans truthfulness because he never does one thing with the intention of achieving another in the first place.

So, God has the ability to create miracles at the hands if a false prophet, and on the Ashari view, whether He would choose to do so or not is entirely arbitrary: no one act is more preferable or likely than the other with respect to His nature and perfection.

And as Ibn Taymiyya says, appealing to "necessity" to justify knowing one thing over another, when theres no objective difference between the two whatsoever, ultimately destroys any reliable basis for knowledge.

 " " 

Finally, one of the most common responses they present is that if false prophets were allowed to perform miracles, it would undermine Gods ability to reveal truth to humanity.

They reason that if false prophets could perform miracles, it would blur the line between truth and falsehood, making it impossible for God to guide people to the truth through miracles.

So they argue that this would compromise Gods omnipotence, as it would entail that He is unable to demonstrate the truthfulness of a prophet, and therefore, it could never happen.

However, this argument falls apart when examined against their broader theological framework. Heres why:

1.       

The first issue is that the Ashari view itself makes the very act of demonstrating a prophets truthfulness through miracles impossible. Their framework denies that God acts with purpose or intention, which means there is no reason behind any action He performs. But for miracles to serve as evidence of prophethood, there must be a purposeful link between the miracle and the claim it validates.

For example, if a king sends a messenger with a unique seal to prove his authority, the seal serves as evidence only because the king intentionally assigned it that role. Without intention, the seal is meaningless.

Similarly, miracles cannot inherently signify truthfulness unless God intentionally creates them for that purpose.

Since the Ashari view denies that God acts with reasons, it follows that demonstrating truthfulness through miracles is inherently impossible in their framework. And if an act is inherently impossible, divine power does not "attach" to itjust as it does not "attach" to creating a square circle.

This makes their argument self-destruct. If their framework already excludes the possibility of demonstrating truth through miracles, then allowing miracles for false prophets would not compromise Gods power, as it cannot attach to something impossible in the first place.

2.    () 

When confronted with the problem of intentionality, some Asharis respond by appealing to talazumthe idea that one thing can logically necessitate another. 

They argue that miracles could inherently serve as evidence of truthfulness through a built-in necessary connection, without requiring any purposeful act from God. For example, they might say that just as knowledge necessitates life, a miracle could necessitate the truthfulness of a prophets claim.

But this argument fails because miracles do not intrinsically signify truthfulness. Their role as evidence of genuine prophethood relies on being intentionally assigned that role by God. Without such purpose or reason, a miracle could just as easily be a random act of power, with no connection to the truth of a prophets claim.

Think of it like a flag on a battlefield. A flag might signify victory, but only if it is intentionally raised for that purpose. If the flag is raised randomly or blown into the air by the wind, it no longer serves as a meaningful indicator of victory.

Similarly, for miracles to signify the truthfulness of a prophet, there must be a purposeful act linking the miracle to its role as evidence.

Without any such reasons behind God's actions, there is no necessary connection between miracles and truthfulness. Their appeal to talazum collapses because the connection between a miracle and its meaning is not intrinsic; it must be purposefully assigned.

3.       

This "compromised power" response also leads to a deeper dilemma: is it possible for God to allow a false prophet to perform miracles, or not?

They are left with two options, both of which are problematic:

)            :

This position is correct, because it aligns with the idea that Gods wisdom and perfection would never permit the validation of falsehood. However, it also contradicts their framework, which denies that any act can be inherently unbefitting or inappropriate for God.

For them, all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God's perfection, so they cannot consistently claim that God would never allow miracles at the hands of a false prophet without such an act being impossible in itself.

)           :

This position compromises Gods omnipotence by making something possible in itself (creating miracles at the hands of a false prophet) impossible for God. It implies a deficiency in divine power.
----------------------------------

In short, the Ashari stance on the arbitrariness of Gods actions, along with their belief that no possible act is "unbefitting" of Him, leads to serious foundational issues with the concepts of truth and divine guidance."

"Dismissing The Scholars for Lacking Expertise in Nawzil  A Surr Principle Repacked

Shaykh @Arafatbinhassan Al-Muammad, may Allh preserve him was asked: 

Question: May Allh reward you, noble Shaykh. A questioner asks: What is meant by a Nzilah?

Answer: A Nzilah is an event that affects the Muslims at large, requiring the senior scholars to examine it. They study its reality, how it has arisen, what aspects relate to the Sharah, and what ruling (fatw) applies. An example is COVID. It was a widespread pandemic that affected Muslims everywhere, so scholars had to determine the appropriate rulings. A Nzilah is thus a new occurrence for people, often involving hardship, and scholars must clarify the rulings that apply to the Muslims in such a situation.

They first understand it accurately, identify the point of dispute, research it, compare it with similar cases, then consult one another and issue a ruling.

Issues like mosques, distancing, masks, mixing or avoiding mixing, all of these are religious matters. So whom do we ask? We ask the specialists, and they are the scholars.

When the plague struck the Levant (Ash-Shm), what did Umar do? He gathered the scholars. He met with the Immigrants (Muhjirn), then the Supporters (Anr), then the elders of Quraysh who had accepted Islam at the Conquest (of Makkah). He listened to all of them and reached a conclusion. He was the Leader of The Believers (Amrul-Muminn), and when he gives a command, it must be obeyed.

When he gave his ruling, one Companion disagreedAb Ubaydah ibn Al-Jarr, who was the governor over the Levant in the place where the plague was, and he died because of this plague, may Allh be pleased with him. He said: O Leader of The Believers, are you fleeing from the decree of Allh? Umar replied: If only someone other than you had said it, O Ab Ubaydah. Meaning: with your knowledge and virtue, you say such a statement? If someone else had said it, we might have accepted it from him, but from you we do not accept that you say this. Yeswe are fleeing from the decree of Allh to the decree of Allh.

As Umar explained, Ab Ubaydah accepted it. Then Abdur-Ramn ibn Awf arrived and said: I have knowledge of this, and he mentioned the adth: if you hear of a plague in a land, do not enter it, and if you are in it, do not leave. Thus Umars decision aligned with the Sunnah.

In such matters, we return to the scholars.

Now some of them have appeared who say: do not return to the scholars because they do not understand medicine. What benefit is there in referring to them? This is dangerous speech! This effectively dismisses the scholars rulings in Nawzil. Do you even realize what you are saying? Because when you say, Do not return to the scholars in medical matters since they do not understand medicine, then finedoes any of us claim that Shaykh Al-Fawzn is a medical, agricultural or technical expert?

Based on the fatw of the one who made this statement, then we should not return to Al-Fawzn in any Nzilah, because he does not understand medicine, nor agriculture, nor computers, nor satellite technology, and so on.

What is the conclusion of this? That we do not return to the scholars in Nawzil. The conclusion of such thinking is to abandon the scholars and turn instead to doctors and engineers. This is dangerous speech!

Moreover, he did not stop at that. He also says: Likewise, they do not go beyond the statement of the rulerif the ruler says something, they will say we hear and obey. Is this the way of our scholars? That whatever the ruler says, they hear and obey? This is false and incorrect. If the ruler makes a mistake, they do not obey himthere is no obedience to creation in disobedience to the Creator. If it is an error, they advise the ruler and speak to him (privately), because this is the methodology of Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jamah. 

continued"

"So we rejected this statement and said: this is a dangerous statement. Its implication is that you are nullifying the Fatw of the scholars in Nawzil. If you see that there is a mistake, and you are a Salaf who respects the scholars and also respects the rulers, then write to them. Present your viewpoint only. But to establish this as a general principlethis is false and wrong! 

This resembles the approach of the Surrs and Qubs during the Gulf crisis, who said: Our scholars do not understand current affairswe understand current affairs. America entered, Europe did such-and-such, and you, O scholarslike ibn Bzyou are only with the rulers, obeying them. The same wordsonly the people and circumstances have changed. There it was a Gulf crisis related to the entry of disbelievers (to the lands of the Muslims); here it is a COVID crisis related to medicine.

Furthermore, this man (making these claims) works in medicine. If we look at his specialization, do you work in every field of medicine and every specialty? No. Then speak only within your level. That is one point.

Secondly, do you want the entire Ummah to abandon the doctors of the world and the consultants, and instead follow your opinion? This is dangerous speech! It is unclear what this person intends with such statements. 

Despite that, he has been advised, and we have advised him before. We even sent to him years ago when he issued a Fatw. Look at this Fatwmany people may not even be aware of it. He issued a ruling that social distancing was Minor Shirk. That means Al-Fawzn has fallen into Shirk, the Muft has fallen into Shirk, and our scholars such as Ubayd and others have fallen into Shirk. And we, the students of knowledge, have all fallen into Shirk. And the Fatw of the scholars who permitted distancing are, according to him, Fatw of Shirk.

So who is behind you that you speak with such words? What do you want, that you strike at the scholars by claiming they have fallen into (minor) Shirk, that they should not be referred to, and that they neither understand nor comprehend? This is what he says in the audio recordings. 

Some have not even listened to these recordings yet and do not want to listenand this is a calamity. You find a person plunging headlong into Fitnah while knowing nothing. And if you say to him, Come, let us sit and clarify things for you, he refuses to listen. This is blind partisanship. 

As for us, we clarify the error. Whoever is guided, then all praise is due to Allh, and whoever goes astray, then it is upon himself.

Shaykh Abdul-Azz ibn Bz was asked on his official site: Should we refer only to doctors, or to the scholars? He replied: No. One must return to the scholar. If the matter were purely medical, then yeswe would return to the doctors; because they are the people of specialization. But this concerns a matter connected to medicine and also to the Sharah.

Does Al-Fawzn immediately issue a ruling when a purely medical issue comes to him? You think highly of yourself and consider yourself an expert. But when this scholarAl-Fawzn, the Muft, Al-Abbd, Rab, and othersare asked about a worldly technical matter such as medicine or agriculture, do they refer to specialists or not? They do refer to the specialists. This shows that you do not understand how the scholars issue Fatw.

I have already explained this in detail in one of the refutationsthe methodology of the scholar in giving Fatwand it is published on my channel. Yet some people ignore all of this and do not want to listen. 

Continued"

"So beware, may Allh bless you, and do not rush. Be a student of knowledge who has the ability to evaluate and critique. If you are not able, then remain silentthat is my advice to you. Many Salafs carry knowledge, love knowledge, and attend lessons, but they have not reached the stage of being a master of this knowledge, capable of assessing and critiquing. If you are not at that level, then remain silent during times of Fitnah and dont delve into them. Ask: what did the scholars say? We stand behind our scholars and follow them.

You do not have the ability to independently assess. But instead, you immediately become extreme, align yourself with a certain person, and defend his opinion while you do not even know what is in his statement, nor have you listened, nor understood what the criticism against him actually is. It is not appropriate for you, as a student of knowledge, to behave like this. Fear Allh regarding yourself. 

Shaykh Abdullh Al-Bukhr advised the youth across the world: do not engage in the COVID fitnahremain silent. Whoever wants to take the vaccine may take it in concurrence with the scholars, and whoever refrains and says, I do not want it, may refrain. We do not say to the people that it is obligatory to take the vaccine. If someone says, I do not want to take it, then that is up to him. But to incite against the scholarsclaiming they do not understand, do not comprehend, that one should not ask them, and what is the benefit of asking them?this is what he says: What is the benefit of asking the scholars? They will not give you anything. We seek refuge in Allh! This is the speech of the people of innovation and misguidance.

He even says: no matter how knowledgeable a scholar becomes, he will simply follow the ruler. This is dangerous speech! It reduces our scholars to mere followers of others, stripping them of value, of the worth of their Fatw, and denying that they understand Nawzil.

We wrote and corresponded, but unfortunately they did not take the advice of Shaykh Abdullh. When the Shaykh saw that this person persisted, he spoke in a way intended to discipline him somewhat and make him recognize his own level. Yet they still did not respond. They continue to transmiteven the words of disbelieversacross their networks, pages, and websites. They did not benefit from Shaykh Abdullhs advice, nor from their mistakes, nor did they retract from their claim that the scholars do not understand or comprehend.

If I were to say to any one of you now, even the least among you, you do not understand, you do not comprehend, would you accept that from me? So how then when this is said about the senior Salaf scholars of the Ummahthat they do not understand, do not comprehend, and should not be referred to? What is the benefit of returning to them? We seek refuge in Allh from such misguidance!
